name: Fashion Designer
summary: A creator of apparel lines who sketches silhouettes, selects fabrics, and directs runway presentations—professionalized in Paris couture houses and now spanning fast fashion to luxury ateliers.
duties: Research trends, develop collections, fit samples, present seasons, and liaise with production.
skills: Draping, illustration, textile knowledge, brand vision, deadline management.
tools: Dress forms, CAD, mood boards, sample rooms.
regions: Paris, Milan, New York, London, Lagos fashion weeks.

events.label: Dior New Look
events.description: Postwar silhouette revolution cements designer-as-celebrity model.

educationPath: Fashion school, atelier internships, competition prizes.
modernSectors: Luxury houses, contemporary labels, costume, celebrity custom.

name: Fashion Model
summary: A professional wearer of garments for runway, editorial, and advertising—industrialized by agencies and magazine culture as the mobile mannequin of seasonal style.
duties: Castings, fittings, walk runway, pose shoots, travel, and maintain book visibility.
skills: Runway walk, posing, resilience, punctuality, personal branding.
tools: Comp cards, digitals, heels, go-see maps, social portfolios.
regions: Paris agencies, New York Fashion Week, emerging markets scouting.
originLabel: Early fashion photography and mannequin parades

events.label: Supermodel era
events.description: Celebrity models negotiate multimillion contracts beyond runway day rates.

educationPath: Agency scouting, runway workshops, fitness discipline.
modernSectors: Runway, e-commerce fit, fitness, influencer crossover.

name: Makeup Artist
summary: A cosmetic applicator for beauty, fashion, film, and bridal clients—elevated from department counters to backstage teams at global fashion weeks.
duties: Prep skin, execute looks, maintain kits, collaborate with stylists, and touch up sets.
skills: Color theory, hygiene, speed, trend fluency, client consultation.
tools: Brush sets, airbrush, palettes, sanitizers, LED mirrors.
regions: Hollywood, K-beauty, Paris couture backstage, bridal markets.
originLabel: Theatrical and Max Factor film cosmetics

events.label: Celebrity MUA brands
events.description: Named artists launch product lines and tutorial media empires.

educationPath: Beauty school, backstage assisting, certification courses.
modernSectors: Editorial, bridal, SFX overlap, retail artistry, content creators.

name: Fashion Stylist
summary: A look curator who assembles outfits for shoots, celebrities, and retail campaigns—distinct from store clerks by editorial eye and pull-letter access to showrooms.
duties: Source clothing, fit talent, coordinate returns, build mood boards, and on-set dressing.
skills: Trend synthesis, networking, budgeting, diplomacy with houses, speed.
tools: Pull letters, steamer, garment bags, Pinterest boards, rental accounts.
regions: New York editorial, LA red carpet, European magazine culture.

events.label: Celebrity stylist rise
events.description: Red carpet economy professionalizes personal styling as standalone career.

educationPath: Assistant years, fashion PR crossover, portfolio building.
modernSectors: Editorial, advertising, personal styling, e-commerce lookbooks.

name: Perfumer
summary: An artist-chemist who composes fragrances from essential oils and synthetics—Grasse tradition meets global luxury conglomerate labs creating signature scents.
duties: Blend accords, evaluate maceration, brief marketing teams, and reformulate for compliance.
skills: Olfactory memory, chemistry basics, creative brief interpretation, regulatory knowledge.
tools: GC-MS, scent organ, blotters, formula databases.
regions: Grasse, Geneva, New York fine fragrance houses.

events.label: No. 5 launch
events.description: Chanel No. 5 popularizes designer-owned fragrance as fashion extension.

educationPath: ISIPCA or house schools, apprentice nose years.
modernSectors: Fine fragrance, niche indie, home scent, celebrity licensing.

name: Cosmetologist
summary: A licensed beauty practitioner covering hair, skin, and nails in salon settings—regulated profession as chemical services and sanitation rules professionalized beauty parlors.
duties: Cut and color hair, perform facials and nails per scope, sanitize stations, and retail products.
skills: Sanitation, chemical timing, client consultation, state law compliance.
tools: Shears, color bowls, esthetic machines, autoclave.
regions: U.S. state-licensed salons, European coiffeurs, Asian beauty chains.

events.label: Permanent wave popularity
events.description: Chemical hair services expand salon revenue and training requirements.

educationPath: Cosmetology license hours, board exams, CE credits.
modernSectors: Full-service salons, suite rentals, cruise ship spas.

name: Hairdresser
summary: A hair cutter and stylist serving daily grooming and special events—from neighborhood barber-cosmetology overlap to session stylists on fashion shoots.
duties: Consult on style, cut, color, blow-dry, and maintain client records.
skills: Cutting geometry, color theory, conversation, time management.
tools: Shears, clippers, foils, dryers, styling products.
regions: Global high streets, session stylists in media capitals.

events.label: Vidal Sassoon revolution
events.description: Wash-and-wear cuts link hairstyling to modernist fashion silhouettes.

educationPath: Salon apprenticeship, cosmetology license, advanced color certs.
modernSectors: Salons, editorial session, film, platform education.

name: Manicurist
summary: A nail care specialist who shapes, polishes, and extends nails—salon staple intensified by gel, acrylic, and nail-art social media trends.
duties: Sanitize tools, shape nails, apply polish or extensions, and retail care products.
skills: Fine motor control, design, sanitation, client comfort.
tools: Files, UV lamps, acrylic monomer, nail art brushes.
regions: Korean nail art studios, U.S. strip-mall salons, luxury hotel spas.
originLabel: Cutex and commercial nail lacquer era

events.label: Instagram nail art
events.description: Social media accelerates intricate design trends and indie product lines.

educationPath: Nail tech license, manufacturer certification workshops.
modernSectors: Nail salons, mobile services, editorial hand modeling overlap.

name: Esthetician
summary: A skin-care therapist performing facials, hair removal, and non-medical treatments—bridging dermatology referrals and spa relaxation economies.
duties: Analyze skin, perform treatments, recommend home care, document contraindications.
skills: Anatomy basics, sanitation, machine protocols, sales without overpromising.
tools: Steamers, microdermabrasion, wax, LED panels.
regions: Day spas, medi-spa hybrids, resort wellness.

events.label: Medi-spa growth
events.description: Physician oversight models blend clinical and aesthetic services.

educationPath: Esthetician license, advanced machine certs.
modernSectors: Spas, dermatology clinics, cruise ships, product brand educators.

name: Fashion Buyer
summary: A merchant who selects seasonal assortments for department stores and boutiques—power broker between designers and retail floors with OTB budgets.
duties: Attend markets, negotiate terms, plan depth, analyze sell-through, and manage vendors.
skills: Trend forecasting, margin math, negotiation, travel stamina.
tools: OTB spreadsheets, PLM, market appointment calendars.
regions: New York market week, European showrooms, Asian sourcing hubs.

events.label: Fast fashion buying cycles
events.description: Zara model compresses lead times and changes buyer decision rhythm.

educationPath: Fashion merchandising degree, assistant buyer track.
modernSectors: Department stores, specialty chains, e-commerce private label.

name: Pattern Maker
summary: A technical translator who turns designer sketches into graded paper or digital patterns—factory pipeline role essential for consistent fit at scale.
duties: Draft blocks, digitize patterns, grade sizes, mark notches, and sample fit comments.
skills: Flat pattern, draping, CAD, tolerance for iteration.
tools: Pattern paper, Gerber/Lectra, rulers, muslin.
regions: Garment districts NYC, LA, Bangladesh tech offices.

events.label: CAD pattern adoption
events.description: Computer grading speeds production in offshore manufacturing.

educationPath: Patternmaking programs, factory apprenticeship.
modernSectors: Brands, factories, costume shops, athletic wear.

name: Dressmaker
summary: A seamstress focused on women's custom garments and alterations—bridged home sewing and couture before ready-to-wear, still vital for formal and bridal work.
duties: Measure clients, cut fabric, sew fittings, finish hems, and schedule alterations.
skills: Sewing precision, fitting judgment, fabric behavior, client diplomacy.
tools: Sewing machine, serger, irons, mannequin.
regions: Urban alteration shops, bridal districts, home studios.

events.label: Ready-to-wear expansion
events.description: Mass production shrinks custom dressmaking to formal niches.

name: Haute Couture Artisan
summary: A workshop specialist in Paris-certified couture houses executing hand beading, pleating, or featherwork—guardian of legally protected luxury garment construction standards.
duties: Execute designer techniques, maintain atelier quality, mentor apprentices, and meet show deadlines.
skills: Hand sewing excellence, material mastery, patience, discretion.
tools: Needles, specialty pleating boards, feather mounts, toiles.
regions: Paris couture ateliers, Chanel métiers d'art satellites.
originLabel: Chambre Syndicale de la Haute Couture

events.label: Postwar couture revival
events.description: French industry protects artisan ateliers as national prestige export.

name: Textile Designer
summary: A creator of woven, printed, or knit patterns for apparel and interiors—feeds fashion collections from mills in Como, Lyon, and digital print-on-demand studios.
duties: Develop repeats, colorways, liaise with mills, strike-offs, and archive libraries.
skills: Repeat mechanics, color separation, trend research, CAD.
tools: Loom samples, digital printers, swatch archives, Adobe suite.
regions: Italian silk mills, Indian block print, digital print UK.

events.label: Digital textile printing
events.description: Short-run print tech enables micro-collection fabric experimentation.

educationPath: Textile design degree, mill internships.
modernSectors: Fashion brands, home decor, performance fabrics.

name: Fashion Photographer
summary: A lens specialist who shoots clothing narratives for magazines and campaigns—Richard Avedon-era glamour turned into multi-platform content production.
duties: Interpret briefs, light sets, direct models, deliver selects, and manage usage rights.
skills: Lighting, composition, casting eye, production logistics, retouch direction.
tools: DSLR/medium format, strobes, tethering, mood references.
regions: New York, Paris, Milan, Lagos creative scenes.

events.label: Digital capture shift
events.description: Film-to-digital transition speeds editorial turnaround and Instagram-era content.

educationPath: Photo assisting, portfolio building, agency representation.
modernSectors: Editorial, e-commerce, lookbooks, moving image campaigns.

name: Wardrobe Supervisor
summary: A film and theater lead who manages costume inventory, continuity, and quick changes—backstage fashion discipline ensuring performers look correct in every take.
duties: Break down scripts, budget wardrobe, supervise dressers, maintain continuity logs.
skills: Organization, sewing repairs, union rules, collaboration with costume design.
tools: Rack plans, continuity photos, sewing kits, laundry units.
regions: Hollywood studios, Broadway, global location shoots.

events.label: TV production scale
events.description: Episodic television multiplies wardrobe supervisor career slots.

educationPath: Costume PA progression, union wardrobe courses.
modernSectors: Film, TV, theater, touring concerts.

name: Jewelry Designer
summary: A creator of wearable metal and gem compositions—spanning bench artisans to CAD-driven brands selling globally through e-commerce and flagship stores.
duties: Sketch collections, prototype pieces, source stones, manage casting vendors, and market lines.
skills: Design proportion, gemology basics, branding, production costing.
tools: CAD Rhino/Matrix, wax carvers, render engines, sample vaults.
regions: NYC Diamond District, Jaipur, Italian gold manufacturing.

events.label: CAD jewelry revolution
events.description: Digital modeling and 3D printing democratize custom design workflows.

educationPath: Jewelry design programs, bench apprenticeship, GIA electives.
modernSectors: Fine jewelry, fashion jewelry, bespoke bridal, DTC brands.

name: Corsetier
summary: A specialist in structured undergarments who fitted steel-boned corsets—Victorian necessity eased by elastic and cultural rejection of extreme waist training.
duties: Measure torso, draft patterns, fit bones, and adjust lacing tension.
skills: Anatomy, engineering tension, discreet fitting, sewing strength.
tools: Busks, boning, grommets, coutil fabric.
regions: Paris, London Victorian trade, modern burlesque revival niche.

events.label: Flapper undergarment shift
events.description: Boyish silhouettes and elastic girdles reduce everyday corset demand.

name: Lingerie Designer
summary: A designer of intimate apparel balancing fit engineering and aesthetics—industrialized as department store bra counters and global brands scaled sizing science.
duties: Develop bra blocks, select lace, fit models, manage comfort testing, and plan lines.
skills: Fit engineering, textile drape, sizing diversity, brand storytelling.
tools: Bra pattern blocks, fit models, lab stretch tests.
regions: France, UK, Colombia manufacturing, global brands.

events.label: Wonderbra marketing moment
events.description: Mass lingerie campaigns elevate designer roles in intimate apparel.

educationPath: Fashion design with intimate focus, technical design programs.
modernSectors: Brands, private label, adaptive lingerie startups.

name: Fashion Illustrator
summary: An artist who renders garments for presentations and press—dominant before photography and resurging in digital sketching for pre-production and social content.
duties: Illustrate looks, support design meetings, create marketing art, and archive season visuals.
skills: Figure drawing, fabric indication, speed, digital and wet media.
tools: Procreate, markers, watercolor, Wacom tablets.
regions: Design studios, fashion schools, freelance editorial.

events.label: Digital illustration tools
events.description: Tablets and Instagram revive illustrator visibility alongside photography.

name: Fashion Editor
summary: A magazine or digital lead who shapes style coverage, cover choices, and critic voice—gatekeeper role challenged by influencers yet still central at legacy fashion titles.
duties: Assign stories, edit copy, attend shows, negotiate credits, and plan issues.
skills: Taste authority, writing, network, budget, cultural commentary.
tools: CMS, show schedules, PR seating requests, social analytics.
regions: Vogue orbit cities, Condé Nast, independent Substacks.

events.label: Bloggers front row
events.description: Digital creators challenge editor monopoly on show access and narrative.

educationPath: Journalism or fashion studies, editorial assistant ladder.
modernSectors: Print, digital media, brand content studios, consulting.

name: Runway Coordinator
summary: A backstage logistics lead who schedules models, cues music, and manages quick changes during fashion shows—high-pressure production role behind every Fashion Week calendar slot.
duties: Create lineup, manage dressing stations, sync with production, troubleshoot timing.
skills: Calm under chaos, radio discipline, model management, designer diplomacy.
tools: Lineup sheets, headsets, garment racks, show caller scripts.
regions: NYFW, PFW, MFW, LFW production agencies.

events.label: Livestream runway
events.description: Broadcast deadlines add technical sync duties to backstage coordinators.

name: Beauty Influencer
summary: A content creator who monetizes makeup tutorials, reviews, and lifestyle aesthetics—new occupation born of YouTube and TikTok affiliate economies.
duties: Film content, disclose sponsorships, engage community, and negotiate brand deals.
skills: On-camera presence, editing, trend spotting, FTC compliance.
tools: Ring lights, affiliate links, analytics dashboards, PR unboxing.
regions: Global; LA and Seoul beauty content hubs.

events.label: TikTok beauty virality
events.description: Short-form algorithm spikes indie creator careers and product sellouts.

educationPath: Self-taught content, talent management, brand deal lawyers.
modernSectors: Sponsored posts, owned brands, live shopping, podcasting.

name: Nail Technician
summary: A licensed nail professional overlapping manicurist scope with emphasis on technical enhancements and sanitation protocols in regulated salons.
duties: Perform manicures, extensions, removals, and infection-control protocols.
skills: Sculpting, chemistry safety, ergonomics, retail recommendation.
tools: E-files, acrylic systems, ventilation, autoclave.
regions: U.S. nail salons, UK NVQ-certified shops.

events.label: Gel polish mainstream
events.description: Soak-off gels become default salon service driving tech specialization.

educationPath: State nail tech license, brand educator certifications.
modernSectors: Salons, medi-spa nails, editorial session nails.

name: Wig Maker
summary: A artisan who knots human or synthetic hair into lace foundations—for theater, film, medical hair loss, and drag performance with distinct construction methods.
duties: Knot hair, customize hairlines, style units, and maintain client fittings.
skills: Ventilating knots, color blending, durability, discretion for medical clients.
tools: Ventilating needle, lace caps, mannequin heads, steamers.
regions: London West End, Hollywood SFX, medical wig boutiques.

events.label: Synthetic fiber wigs
events.description: Kanekalon and lace-front methods broaden affordable wig markets.

name: Color Consultant
summary: An advisor who analyzes personal coloring to guide wardrobe and makeup palettes—popularized by seasonal color theory books and corporate image consulting.
duties: Drape clients, explain palettes, advise shopping, and train retail staff.
skills: Perception of undertone, teaching, diplomacy, wardrobe psychology.
tools: Drape swatches, color wheels, style guides.
regions: Image consulting firms, department store services.

events.label: Personal branding services
events.description: Executive coaching packages integrate color with LinkedIn-era image.

name: Fragrance Chemist
summary: A lab scientist who formulates stable scent compounds and ensures IFRA compliance—bridging creative briefs from perfumers with manufacturing reproducibility.
duties: Prototype formulas, run stability tests, scale batches, document allergens.
skills: Organic chemistry, GC analysis, regulatory databases, collaboration with marketing.
tools: Lab reactors, GC-MS, IFRA standards library.
regions: IFF, Givaudan, Firmenich global labs.

events.label: IFRA standards
events.description: Industry self-regulation shapes chemist role in safe formulation.

educationPath: Chemistry degree, flavor and fragrance programs.
modernSectors: Fine fragrance, personal care, home care R&D.

name: Custom Dressmaker
summary: A bespoke garment maker serving individual clients with one-off fittings—premium tier above alterations, competing with couture on personalization without syndicate label.
duties: Consult design, toile fittings, hand finishes, and delivery timelines.
skills: Bespoke consultation, advanced sewing, luxury service.
tools: Muslin, hand needles, premium fabrics, appointment calendar.
regions: Bridal ateliers, bespoke districts, online made-to-measure hybrids.

events.label: Made-to-measure tech
events.description: 3D body scans and online configurators extend custom dressmaking reach.

name: Fashion Merchandiser
summary: A planner who allocates product to stores, sets floor presentation, and analyzes margin—connects buying decisions to customer-facing assortment performance.
duties: Plan store sets, manage markdowns, track KPIs, coordinate visual teams.
skills: Analytics, visual taste, cross-functional communication, inventory math.
tools: ERP, planograms, heat maps, markdown simulators.
regions: Retail chains, brand wholesale teams, e-commerce ops.

events.label: Fast fashion replenishment
events.description: Weekly delivery models elevate merchandiser operational centrality.

educationPath: Merchandising degrees, retail management trainee programs.
modernSectors: Apparel chains, department stores, DTC planning.

name: Vintage Clothing Dealer
summary: A curator-seller of secondhand fashion by era—fueled by sustainability discourse and archival designer pieces commanding reseller premiums online.
duties: Source inventory, authenticate labels, price, photograph listings, and ship globally.
skills: Label history, condition grading, trend arbitrage, platform SEO.
tools: Depop, eBay, racks, steamer, authentication guides.
regions: Tokyo vintage, Brooklyn, Paris flea, global online resale.

events.label: Resale platform boom
events.description: The RealReal and Vinted normalize vintage dealing as full-time career.

name: Fashion PR Specialist
summary: A publicist who secures press seating, editorials, and celebrity dressing for labels—backstage gatekeeper of fashion's mutual publicity economy.
duties: Write press releases, manage samples, pitch stories, crisis-manage, and track coverage.
skills: Media relationships, writing, event timing, brand voice, crisis calm.
tools: PR databases, sample logs, seating charts, coverage reports.
regions: NY, Paris, London PR agencies representing designers.

events.label: Influencer PR pivot
events.description: Digital creators join editors on PR seeding lists and front-row politics.

educationPath: PR or fashion communications degrees, agency internships.
modernSectors: Designer PR, celebrity styling PR, in-house brand comms.

name: Clothing Alteration Specialist
summary: A sewist focused on resizing and repairing ready-to-wear—steady neighborhood trade as online sizing inconsistency and sustainability encourage fixing over discarding.
duties: Hem pants, take in waists, replace zippers, and quote turnaround times.
skills: Machine sewing, fitting judgment, quick estimation, customer service.
tools: Industrial machine, serger, iron, pinning forms.
regions: Dry cleaner add-ons, mall alteration kiosks, bridal shops.

events.label: Resale alteration demand
events.description: Thrift flipping trends increase tailoring of secondhand garments.

name: Fabric Sourcer
summary: A supply-chain scout who finds mills and negotiates minimums for fashion brands—critical role as nearshoring and compliance audits reshape textile procurement.
duties: Visit mills, evaluate quality, negotiate MOQs, track lead times, and document compliance.
skills: Textile testing literacy, negotiation, logistics, sustainability standards.
tools: Lab dip approvals, compliance portals, freight calculators.
regions: Italy, China, Turkey, Portugal mill networks.

events.label: Rana Plaza aftermath
events.description: Factory safety scrutiny elevates sourcing transparency duties.

educationPath: Textile science, sourcing trainee roles, compliance workshops.
modernSectors: Brands, buying offices, sustainable material startups.

name: Fashion Trend Forecaster
summary: A researcher who predicts colors, silhouettes, and consumer moods seasons ahead—selling insight reports to brands from trend agencies like WGSN lineage.
duties: Scan culture, compile reports, present to clients, and validate with data.
skills: Cultural anthropology, visual research, presentation, humility about misses.
tools: Trend platforms, runway archives, consumer survey data.
regions: London, Paris, NYC trend agencies.

events.label: Fast fashion forecasting
events.description: Compressed cycles demand continuous micro-trend updates not just seasonal books.

educationPath: Fashion studies, cultural research, agency analyst programs.
modernSectors: Trend agencies, in-house strategy, retail planning.

name: Beauty Pageant Organizer
summary: A producer who runs competition logistics, judging, and sponsorship for pageants—contested cultural institution from Atlantic City spectacles to national broadcast events.
duties: Book venues, recruit contestants, secure sponsors, manage judging, and ensure broadcast.
skills: Event production, diplomacy, marketing, regulatory navigation, talent coaching.
tools: Run-of-show, contracts, ticketing, teleprompter.
regions: U.S. state pageants, Miss Universe franchise countries.

events.label: Protest at Miss America
events.description: Feminist critique spurs evolution of organizer messaging and scholarship emphasis.

name: Embroidery Artisan
summary: A needleworker who decorates fabric with thread, beads, or metallic wire—couture houses and regional folk traditions alike depend on hand embroidery ateliers.
duties: Interpret designer artwork, select threads, execute stitches, and repair vintage pieces.
skills: Stitch vocabulary, patience, color blending, magnification work.
tools: Hoops, tambour hooks, beads, magnification lamps.
regions: India zardozi, Haute couture Paris, Mexican folk blouses.

events.label: Machine embroidery competition
events.description: Schiffli machines handle volume while hand work stays luxury tier.

name: Leather Goods Designer
summary: A creator of bags, belts, and small leather accessories—luxury houses treat leather goods as margin engines with dedicated design studios.
duties: Sketch silhouettes, prototype patterns, select hides, and coordinate hardware.
skills: Pattern engineering, hardware specs, brand codes, costing.
tools: CAD, sample room, hide swatches, edge paint stations.
regions: Florence, Paris maroquinerie, New York accessories brands.

events.label: It-bag marketing
events.description: Designer handbag hype turns leather goods into flagship fashion revenue.

educationPath: Accessory design programs, atelier internships.
modernSectors: Luxury maisons, contemporary brands, independent studios.

name: Milliner
summary: A hat designer and maker for fashion and occasion wear—distinct from generic hatters by focus on feminine formal headwear and runway sculptural pieces.
duties: Block shapes, trim veils and feathers, fit clients, and produce showpieces.
skills: Blocking, proportion for faces, material stiffening, trend awareness.
tools: Hat blocks, steamers, sinamay, feathers, millinery wire.
regions: Royal Ascot culture, Kentucky Derby, Paris couture accessories.
originLabel: Millinery as women's fashion accessory trade

events.label: Everyday hat decline
events.description: Casual dress shrinks daily millinery to occasion and runway niches.

name: Beauty School Instructor
summary: A licensed educator who trains cosmetology students in cutting, coloring, and sanitation—feeds the salon pipeline through accredited vocational programs.
duties: Deliver curriculum, supervise clinic floor, assess competencies, and maintain accreditation records.
skills: Pedagogy, technical mastery, state board prep, safety enforcement.
tools: Syllabi, mannequin heads, clinic stations, LMS.
regions: U.S. beauty academies, UK NVQ providers.

events.label: State hour requirements
events.description: Standardized training hours formalize instructor credential pathways.

educationPath: Master educator license, industry experience years.
modernSectors: Beauty academies, brand educator overlap, online theory courses.

name: Cosmetic Chemist
summary: A formulation scientist who develops skincare, haircare, and color cosmetics—turning lab batches into shelf-stable products under regulatory safety frameworks.
duties: Prototype formulas, stability test, scale manufacturing, document INCI, support claims.
skills: Emulsion chemistry, preservative systems, regulatory dossiers, scale-up.
tools: Lab homogenizers, pH meters, stability chambers, PLM.
regions: L'Oréal, Estée Lauder, indie contract labs.
originLabel: Commercial cold cream and lipstick labs

events.label: EU Cosmetics Directive
events.description: Safety dossiers professionalize cosmetic chemist compliance role in Europe.

educationPath: Chemistry or cosmetic science degree, formulation internships.
modernSectors: R&D labs, contract manufacturing, indie brand consulting.
